issues.description: Loop range changed from `0..n` to `0..=n` (inclusive). When `i == n`, both `params[i]` and `vertex[i]` are out-of-bounds accesses (both have length `n`, valid indices 0..n-1). This will panic at runtime on every invocation of `build_simplex` with any non-empty input.
issues.suggested_fix: Revert the loop range back to `0..n`. The original range was correct: a simplex in N dimensions requires exactly N+1 vertices (1 initial + N perturbations, one per dimension).

issues.description: Even ignoring the panic, the inclusive range `0..=n` would push N+1 perturbed vertices plus the initial point, yielding N+2 total vertices. A Nelder-Mead simplex in N dimensions requires exactly N+1 vertices. The docstring still correctly says 'Creates N+1 vertices' but the code contradicts it. The `argmin` NelderMead solver will likely reject or produce incorrect results with a malformed simplex.
issues.suggested_fix: Use `0..n` to generate exactly N perturbations, yielding N+1 total vertices as required.

issues.description: The diff changes `build_simplex` to require a third `scale: f64` parameter, but the call site at line 568 (`build_simplex(initial, &problem.auto_params)`) and three test call sites (lines 2171, 2190, 2215) are not updated. This will cause a compilation error.
issues.suggested_fix: Update all call sites to pass the `scale` argument. The existing callers should pass `0.1` to preserve current behavior, or use the appropriate scale value for the context.

issues.description: `validate_simplex` is defined but never called anywhere in the codebase. It adds no runtime value as dead code and will trigger a compiler warning (`unused function`).
issues.suggested_fix: Either integrate `validate_simplex` as a debug assertion at the end of `build_simplex` (e.g., `debug_assert!(validate_simplex(&simplex, n))`) or in callers, or remove it until it's needed. If kept, consider making it `#[cfg(test)]` or `#[allow(dead_code)]`.

opus_strategic.summary: This diff contains two critical runtime bugs and one compilation error. The loop range change from `0..n` to `0..=n` will cause an index out-of-bounds panic on every call (accessing `params[n]` and `vertex[n]` where both have length `n`), and even conceptually produces the wrong number of simplex vertices (N+2 instead of N+1) for the Nelder-Mead algorithm. Additionally, the new `scale` parameter is added to the function signature but the four existing call sites are not updated, causing a build failure. The parameterization of the hardcoded `0.1` scale factor is a sound improvement, but the loop range must remain `0..n` and all callers must be updated. The new `validate_simplex` function is never called and should either be wired in as a debug assertion or deferred.

issues.description: The loop was changed from `0..n` to `0..=n`. On the final iteration `i == n`, the code accesses `params[n]` and `vertex[n]`. Both slices have length `n` (valid indices 0..n-1), so this is an unconditional index-out-of-bounds panic for any call with `n >= 1`. The existing `simplex_has_n_plus_1_vertices` test directly calls `build_simplex` with 1D, 2D, and 3D inputs — all three cases will now panic instead of asserting. Additionally, even if the panic were somehow avoided, the loop would add n+1 perturbed vertices instead of n, producing a simplex with n+2 total vertices, violating the Nelder-Mead invariant and contradicting the docstring which still says 'Creates N+1 vertices.'
issues.suggested_fix: Revert the loop bound to `0..n`. If the intent was to always produce n+2 vertices, the docstring, the `validate_simplex` check, and any callers expecting a specific size must all be updated consistently — but for standard Nelder-Mead, N+1 vertices is correct.

issues.description: The signature of `build_simplex` now requires three arguments `(initial, params, scale)`, but the only production call site at line 568 is `build_simplex(initial, &problem.auto_params)` with two arguments. This is a compile error that prevents the entire test suite from running. No test covers the new `scale` parameter at the call site.
issues.suggested_fix: Update the call at line 568 to pass an explicit scale, e.g. `build_simplex(initial, &problem.auto_params, 0.1)`, preserving the previous behavior. Add a named constant `const DEFAULT_SIMPLEX_SCALE: f64 = 0.1;` to document the prior default.

issues.description: `validate_simplex` is added but has zero call sites and zero tests. As dead code it contributes no safety net and will be removed by the compiler with a warning. Its correctness (it checks `simplex.len() == n + 1` and `all(|v| v.len() == n)`) is never exercised, so any future caller relying on it gets no confidence from the test suite.
issues.suggested_fix: Either add a `#[test]` covering `validate_simplex` directly (correct / off-by-one / mismatched inner length cases) and wire it into `build_simplex` with a `debug_assert!`, or remove it until it is needed.

issues.description: The new `scale` parameter is untested for meaningful boundary values. A `scale = 0.0` produces a degenerate simplex where every vertex is identical to the initial point; passing that to Nelder-Mead causes the algorithm to stall or report false convergence immediately. A negative `scale` moves all perturbations in the same direction, which may not be caught by bound-clamping. Neither case has a test.
issues.suggested_fix: Add tests: (1) `scale = 0.0` → assert that `validate_simplex` returns false or that the solver detects degeneracy; (2) `scale = 1.0` → all vertices clamped to their bound extremes but still distinct; (3) negative scale → clarify whether it is legal (add a `debug_assert!(scale > 0.0)` if not). Also update `simplex_has_n_plus_1_vertices` to pass an explicit `scale` argument once the compile error is fixed.

sonnet_test_analyst.summary: The diff introduces two blocking defects that together would make the entire test suite fail to compile and run. First, the loop bound change from `0..n` to `0..=n` in `build_simplex` is an unconditional index-out-of-bounds panic: the final iteration (`i == n`) accesses `params[n]` on a slice of length `n`, crashing every call including the existing `simplex_has_n_plus_1_vertices` unit test. Second, the new `scale: f64` parameter is not supplied at the single production call site (line 568), which is a compile error preventing any tests from running. The diff also introduces `validate_simplex` as dead code with no tests and no call sites. Two suggestions round out the review: `scale = 0.0` produces a degenerate simplex that would silently poison Nelder-Mead convergence and is not guarded by any test, and the existing vertex-count test needs to be updated to pass the new `scale` argument once the blocking issues are resolved.

issues.description: The loop was changed from `0..n` to `0..=n`, making it iterate n+1 times (i = 0..=n). Inside the loop, `params[i]` is accessed, but `params` has length `n` (same as `initial`). When `i == n`, this is an out-of-bounds index access that will **panic at runtime**. The original `0..n` was correct: it produced exactly n+1 simplex vertices (1 initial + n perturbations). With `0..=n`, it would try to produce n+2 vertices and panic before finishing. Every call to `build_simplex` with any non-zero-dimensional problem will crash.
issues.suggested_fix: Revert the loop bound to `for i in 0..n`. The N+1 vertex count (1 initial + N perturbations) is the correct Nelder-Mead simplex size for N dimensions, and the `validate_simplex` function added in this same diff confirms this (`simplex.len() == n + 1`).

issues.description: `scale: f64` is accepted without any validation. Three dangerous cases: (1) `scale == 0.0` produces a degenerate simplex where all n perturbation vertices are identical to the initial point after clamping — Nelder-Mead cannot explore and will either stall or return the initial point as the 'best' solution silently; (2) `scale < 0.0` makes `delta` negative so `vertex[i] += delta` moves below `lo`, and `clamp` collapses all perturbed vertices to `lo`, again producing a degenerate or misleadingly small simplex; (3) `scale` being NaN or infinite propagates into `delta`, producing NaN/inf vertex coordinates that corrupt the cost function.
issues.suggested_fix: Assert or return an error if `scale <= 0.0 || !scale.is_finite()` before the loop. A reasonable guard: `assert!(scale > 0.0 && scale.is_finite() && scale <= 1.0, "scale must be in (0, 1]");`

issues.description: `validate_simplex` is defined but never called anywhere in the diff or (per codebase search) in the file. It appears to have been added as a defensive check, but since it is never invoked, it provides no protection and will generate a `dead_code` compiler warning. Critically, if it had been called after `build_simplex`, it would have immediately caught the `0..=n` off-by-one introduced in this same diff (the returned simplex has n+2 vertices, which `validate_simplex` checks against n+1).
issues.suggested_fix: Call `debug_assert!(validate_simplex(&simplex, n), "simplex has wrong shape: {} vertices for {} dims", simplex.len(), n);` at the end of `build_simplex` before the `return simplex` (or just before returning), or add a `#[cfg(test)]` attribute if it is intentionally test-only.

sonnet_robustness.summary: The diff introduces one blocking runtime panic and two robustness gaps. The critical bug is a loop-bounds change from `0..n` to `0..=n`: because `params[i]` is accessed inside the loop and `params` has exactly `n` elements, the loop will index `params[n]` on its final iteration and panic for any problem with at least one dimension. Ironically, the `validate_simplex` function added in the same diff correctly encodes the n+1 invariant, but is never called, so it provides no protection. Additionally, the new `scale` parameter has no validation—zero, negative, NaN, or infinite values silently produce degenerate or corrupted simplex vertices, which would cause the Nelder-Mead solver to stall or return garbage without any error signal.
